overview:
The NCP-OUSD certification validates a professional's ability to develop and implement 3D workflows using Universal Scene Description (OpenUSD). It tests the candidate's mastery of the USD core architecture, including the composition engine, schema design, and the integration of USD assets within the NVIDIA Omniverse ecosystem for real-time collaboration and simulation.

This exam is designed for technical artists, pipeline engineers, and software developers who are building scalable 3D pipelines. Prerequisites include a strong understanding of 3D coordinate systems, basic Python programming, and familiarity with 3D asset exchange formats.
recertification_info: Retake the current version of the exam or a newer version released by NVIDIA before the expiration date.

official_resources.title: NVIDIA Deep Learning Institute (DLI) USD Courses

preparation_tips: Master the 'Composition' concepts: understand the strength ordering of Opinions (LIVPS).
Practice creating custom USD Schemas and understand the difference between Concrete and Abstract schemas.
Deep dive into USD layering and the use of sublayers and payloads for memory optimization.
Implement a basic USD pipeline using Python to automate scene assembly.
Study the USD Prim hierarchy and how attributes/relationships are managed.
Experiment with USD variants to create multiple versions of an asset within a single file.
Learn the specifics of the USD Stage and how to traverse the scene graph using the USD API.
Understand the role of the USD Hydra rendering framework and how it interacts with Omniverse.
Practice converting assets from traditional formats (FBX, OBJ) into USD using NVIDIA Omniverse Connectors.
Review the use of USD plugins and the extension system in NVIDIA Omniverse.
exam_day_tips: Carefully read the composition order questions, as the sequence of opinions is critical.
Manage your time strictly; prioritize the Fundamentals and Composition sections as they carry the most weight.
Look for keywords regarding 'Stronger' vs 'Weaker' opinions in layering scenarios.
Ensure you are comfortable with Python syntax for USD API calls.
Double-check the difference between a 'Sdf' (Layer) and a 'Usd' (Stage) object in the context of the questions.
recommended_experience: 2+ years of experience in 3D pipeline development or technical artistry, with at least 6 months of hands-on experience with OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse.
